request.contentBrief: An operations view of the OpenDesign template estate: one design system rendered across 5 surfaces with 65 live worlds, monitored for quality. KPI row covers worlds live, surfaces, quality-gate pass rate, and open audit findings. Panels track compose-run activity across the three routes (COMPOSE, BORROW, AUDIT), quality-gate pipeline outcomes (certifier, validate_fill rounds, verify rig), per-surface world counts, and audit findings by severity from the design-audit pilot. The one flagged anomaly is a real tension from the run logs (the validate_fill transport serialization issue that forced a domain-function fallback). Synthetic illustrative series expected; real anchors are 65 worlds, 5 surfaces, the Jul 15 borrow pilot and Jul 18 audit pilot dates, and the audit pilot's finding counts (1 critical, 0 major, 1 minor across two targets).

error.message: pinTemplateId "db-ai-risk-command-centre" is not a live dashboard template.
remediation: Pin one of this surface's template ids/experienceIds (typically from a previous call's alternatives), or drop pinTemplateId to let scoring decide.

disposition: BLOCKED — the pinned world exists as a shipped live world but has never been templatized (no *.worldtemplate.manifest.ts, no fill schema, no Template component in experiences/dashboards/db-ai-risk-command-centre/). The only live dashboard template is cockpit (db-model-monitoring-cockpit), whose grammar is precision-grid, so composing on it would misrepresent calm-command in the bake-off. No skeleton was returned, therefore no fill was authored and no validate_fill loop was run. See notes.md.
